glosses: Tonight the moonlight is very fine.
I had not seen it for more than thirty years;
seeing it today, my spirits are extraordinarily refreshed.
Only now do I realize that these past thirty-odd years I have been in a daze;
yet I must be very careful.
Otherwise, why would the Zhao family's dog have given me those two looks?
I have good reason for my fear.
No moonlight at all tonight — I know something is wrong.
Going out this morning, carefully, Zhao Guiweng's expression was strange: as if he feared me, as if he meant to harm me.
There were seven or eight others too, whispering head-to-head about me.
And they were afraid I would see them.
Everyone along the road was like this.
The fiercest of them opened his mouth wide and grinned at me;
a chill ran from my head down to my heels, for I knew their arrangements were already in place.
